mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.54% is near zero, indicating a post-crisis accommodation regime. A sustained period without rate increases (FEDFUNDS flat or declining) signals continued monetary ease, which reduces real discount rates and compresses long-term Treasury yields downward as investors seek duration.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 15 basis points over 20 trading days despite FEDFUNDS remaining below 1%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 145,303k persons shows robust labor market strength. Sustained month-over-month growth in PAYEMS above 150k signals tight labor conditions that increase wage pressure and inflation expectations, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as investors demand higher compensation for inflation risk.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 150k persons in a single month
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls more than 10 basis points over 20 trading days despite PAYEMS growth exceeding 150k, or correlation between monthly PAYEMS changes and 20-day Treasury yield changes is negative (r < -0.1) over any 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 469,092M dollars and housing starts (HOUST) at 1,090k units indicate robust aggregate demand. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, it signals strong economic momentum that raises inflation expectations and long-term real rates, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 469,092M and HOUST at 1,090k as of 2016-12
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls more than 15 basis points over 20 trading days when both RSAFS and HOUST grow month-over-month, or joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 103.86 (index 2017=100) reflects near-trend supply capacity. A sustained decline in INDPRO over 3 months signals supply-side weakness that reduces potential output growth and inflation, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as growth expectations compress.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 20 basis points over 60 trading days despite 3-month INDPRO decline, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 242.348 (index 1982-1984=100) shows moderate price growth. When year-over-year CPI growth exceeds 2.5%, it signals inflation acceleration that forces the Fed to tighten policy expectations, raising 10-year Treasury yields as investors price in higher future short rates.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls more than 10 basis points over 20 trading days when YoY CPI exceeds 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) at 4.7% is near full employment. When UNRATE falls below 4.5%, it signals labor market tightness that accelerates wage growth and inflation expectations, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as the Fed is expected to tighten monetary policy.
regime_hint: tight labor market with inflation risk
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls more than 15 basis points over 20 trading days when UNRATE drops below 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 16,727B (chained 2017 dollars) shows solid growth. When quarterly GDP growth accelerates above 2.5% annualized and PAYEMS simultaneously grows above 150k monthly, it signals strong synchronized demand that raises inflation and real rate expectations, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher.
candidate_trigger: Quarterly GDP growth > 2.5% annualized AND monthly PAYEMS growth > 150k in the same quarter
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls more than 20 basis points over 60 trading days when both GDP growth exceeds 2.5% and PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k, or joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) move together in demand-driven cycles. When INDPRO growth lags RSAFS growth by more than 1 percentage point over 3 months, it signals supply constraints that limit output expansion and raise inflation expectations, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise.
candidate_trigger: 3-month RSAFS growth exceeds 3-month INDPRO growth by > 1 percentage point
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.86 and RSAFS at 469,092M as of 2016-12
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls more than 20 basis points over 60 trading days when RSAFS growth exceeds INDPRO growth by > 1pp, or joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
